title: Tag a Release
description:
## Mark a Version

Releases are usually marked with a Git tag following semantic versioning.

A repository with a commit is ready at **`/root/tagrepo`**.

**Your task:** create a tag named **`v1.0.0`** on the latest commit.

> 💡 `git tag v1.0.0` creates a lightweight tag; `git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "release"` creates an annotated one. Either satisfies this task. List tags with `git tag`.

hints.title: Create the tag
hints.body: An annotated tag stores a message and author; a lightweight tag is just a name pointing at a commit.

setup_commands.command: cd /root/tagrepo && echo hello > file.txt && git add file.txt && git commit -m init

validation.description: Checks that a tag named v1.0.0 exists in /root/tagrepo.
